entry.title: NY Times: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:

  • Russia Seeks Buyers for Plundered Ukraine Grain, U.S. Warns

entry.title: Washington Post: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Britain’s Boris Johnson to face no-confidence vote from his party
  • Russia-Ukraine war live updates: Britain pledges rocket systems after Putin threatens West

  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more

  • Contact the reader representative

Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.content:
Articles in this issue:

  • ‘Bloody cheek, I’m not ageing’: Simon Hattenstone interviews his 94-year-old mum, Marje
  • A new start after 60: ‘There she was – my actual mother. “You took your time!” she said’
  • US bird flu outbreak: millions of birds culled in ‘most inhumane way available’

  • Thousands of UK workers begin world’s biggest trial of four-day week

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 6 Jun, 2022 published at 2022-06-06 10:15AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Beijing’s foreign ministry in Hong Kong hits back at Western consulates over messages marking Tiananmen Square crackdown anniversary
  • Alleged driver charged after online video shows car mounting safety island, travelling against flow of traffic in Hong Kong

  • Russian general reportedly killed in eastern Ukraine, another high-ranking casualty
  • Russian foreign minister Lavrov’s trip to Serbia cancelled as neighbours close airspace
  • Ukraine’s President Zelensky visits close to war front line near Sievierodonetsk
  • Joe Biden scrambles to avoid Americas Summit flop as boycott threats cloud US plans
  • UK’s embattled Boris Johnson to face confidence vote Monday. Will he survive?
  • Can Joe Biden convince the US’ Middle East allies he still has their back?
  • Ukraine claims its forces control ‘half’ of Sievierodonetsk against Russia
  • UK government accused of trying to deport unaccompanied minors to Rwanda
  • Gulf states condemn Indian official’s ‘Islamophobic’ remarks about the Prophet Mohammed
  • Ethiopia accuses aid agencies of delivering banned equipment to Tigray that could be used by rebels

  • 5 most exciting films premiering in June, from the third Jurassic World movie Dominion, to Pixar’s Lightyear and Netflix sci-fi feature Spiderhead starring Chris Hemsworth
  • The 13 best Japanese restaurants in Hong Kong: Sushi Wadatsumi, Shikigiku Japanese Restaurant, Sushi Saito at Four Seasons Hong Kong, Ryota Kappou Modern and Zuicho are all leading the wave
  • How the high jewellery market boomed in Asia during Covid-19: Chanel, Cartier, Bulgari, Van Cleef & Arpels and Graff used Zoom and private viewings for clients to maintain relationships
  • 7 Indian LGBT icons you need to know: from YouTuber Lilly Singh, Olympian Dutee Chand and gay royal Prince Manvendra Singh Gohil, to transgender activist Laxmi Narayan Tripathi and film director Onir
  • Inside Khloé Kardashian’s multi-million dollar car collection: from Porsche and Ferrari supercars to Lamborghini and Rolls-Royce SUVs to rival Kim – but does Kylie Jenner outshine them both?
  • Who’s the world’s richest DJ in 2022? Net worths, ranked: from Calvin Harris’ hits for Rihanna and Dua Lipa, and Steve Aoki’s NFTs, to Daft Punk’s retirement and David Guetta’s 50 million album sales
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Asahi Shimbun: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • TEPCO issues 1st apology from president for nuclear accident
  • Earlier start to rainy season called for Kanto-Koshin region
  • Increased U.S. military flights into Okinawa anger residents
  • Oldest person to sail Pacific alone keeps his next adventure secret
  • Sapporo rejects holding referendum over bidding for Olympics
  • Work to create Osaka Expo site threatens wild birds on island
  • Tohoku’s only bullfighting event celebrates 40th season
  • COVID-19 cases in Tokyo fall for 23rd day, but 2 related deaths
  • Chinese exiles meet in Tokyo to mark Tiananmen anniversary
  • Tokyo to resume travel subsidy deal for residents from June 10
  • Morning exercise deemed threat to national treasure Hikone Castle
  • Brewery gets the jump with craft beer made from crickets
  • Ministry studies war in Ukraine to prepare Japan for invasion
  • Kishida looks to be first Japanese leader to attend a NATO summit
  • Nippon Ishin aims to win Diet seat in Kyoto at CDP’s expense
  • LDP holds its 1st campaign speech meeting in the metaverse
  • Clean energy strategy to seek ‘maximum’ use of nuclear power
  • Japan blasts North Korea for missile salvo, warns of action
  • Beijing reopens restaurants as new COVID-19 cases drop
  • China says Canadian military jets increased reconnaissance, provocations
  • China launches mission to complete space station assembly
  • Australia says China threatened plane over South China Sea
  • U.S., South Korea fire missiles to sea, matching North’s launches
  • North Korea test-fires salvo of short-range missiles
  • U.S., S. Korean navies end key exercise amid N. Korea tension
  • Asian stocks rise as survey shows Chinese services reviving
  • Philippine volcano spews ash and steam, alarms villagers
  • Hundreds gather to mark Tiananmen anniversary in Taiwan
  • Australian leader’s visit affirms deeper ties to Indonesia
  • British Prime Minister Johnson to face confidence vote
  • Google told to pay Australian politician $515,000 over defamatory YouTube videos
  • Russia hits Kyiv with missiles; Putin warns West on arms
  • Russia says Kyiv strikes destroyed tanks donated by West
  • Putin warns West: Russia will strike harder if longer-range missiles supplied
  • Russian missiles strike Kyiv, shattering sense of calm
  • ‘Building blocks of life’ found in soil taken from asteroid Ryugu
  • Footprints of monsters, energy beam celebrate ‘Shin Ultraman’
  • EDITORIAL: New regulations needed to rein in Apple/Google OS phone duopoly
  • VOX POPULI: As weeds flourish in rainy season, a reflection on what they cover
The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.

entry.title: Korea Herald: 6 Jun, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Seoul population continues fall to below 9.5m; down to 7.2m by 2050: report

  • Yoon vows firm and stern response to any NK provocation

  • NK media outlets remain silent about missile launches
  • N. Korea reports 66,680 new fever cases
  • New GM Korea CEO faces labor disputes, falling sales
  • Samsung’s Lee Jae-yong poised for 2-week trip to Europe
  • [Photo News] Hybrid electric race
  • McDonald’s McWings makes a comeback
  • AI uses digital waste for upcycled clothing design
  • Genesis' global sales exceed 700,000 units
  • Investment banks cut Kospi targets amid high inflation

  • Hana Financial stresses connectivity in vision for 2030
  • Pioneering Korean artists in NY living like family: Kim Whan-ki, Han Yong-jin, Moon Mi-aie
  • ‘Broker’ director Kore-eda aims to break prejudice against mothers who give up kids
  • Cellist Choi Ha-young wins prestigious Queen Elisabeth competition
  • 'The Roundup' tops 9 million viewers
  • [Newsmaker] Queen marks 70 years, vows to keep serving

entry.title: NY Times (Print): 6 Jun, 2022
